events.text: Patient is a 58-year-old Asian female with a BMI of 22.8. Mother had ovarian cancer at age 65. BRCA2 variant of uncertain significance.

At age 56, patient had abnormal screening mammogram leading to biopsy of a left breast mass in the upper outer quadrant (C50.4). Invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), grade 2, ER positive (Allred 7/8), PR weakly positive (Allred 4/8), HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (ratio 2.8), Ki-67 35%.

At age 56, left breast stereotactic biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2, 1.8 cm. ER positive 75%, PR weakly positive 15%, HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 2.8, average HER2 copy number 8.2). Ki-67 35%.
At age 56, screening mammogram showed an irregular 1.7 cm mass in the left upper outer quadrant with associated architectural distortion, BIRADS 4C.
At age 56, staging CT showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease. MRI breast showed a single 1.9 cm enhancing mass with no additional suspicious lesions.
At age 56, stage IIA (T1cN1miM0) left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, HER2-positive, ER-positive.
At age 56, underwent left partial mastectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. Final pathology: 1.8 cm IDC, 1 of 3 sentinel nodes with micrometastasis (0.8 mm), margins negative.
At age 56, received neoadjuvant was not given; proceeded with adjuvant TCHP for 6 cycles followed by trastuzumab to complete 1 year.
At age 56, adjuvant radiation therapy to the left breast and regional nodes, 42.56 Gy in 16 fractions (hypofractionated) with a sequential boost of 10 Gy in 4 fractions.
At age 57, completed adjuvant trastuzumab. Initiated adjuvant anastrozole 1 mg daily.
At age 56, during adjuvant TCHP experienced grade 2 alopecia, grade 1 nausea, and grade 2 fatigue. LVEF remained stable at 58%.
At age 57, routine follow-up. Patient on anastrozole, tolerating well with mild arthralgias. Physical exam unremarkable.
At age 58, patient developed persistent headaches. MRI brain showed 3 enhancing lesions in the cerebellum and right frontal lobe (largest 1.8 cm), consistent with brain metastases.
At age 58, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed new bilateral pulmonary nodules and a single 2.2 cm liver lesion, confirming systemic recurrence.
At age 58, recurrent metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer with brain, lung, and liver metastases. Recurrence 2 years after completing adjuvant therapy.
At age 58, underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to the 3 brain lesions: 20 Gy single fraction to 2 smaller lesions, 18 Gy single fraction to the 1.8 cm cerebellar lesion.
At age 58, initiated tucatinib 300 mg twice daily plus trastuzumab and capecitabine (tucatinib combination) given brain metastases and HER2CLIMB trial data.
At age 58, developed grade 2 diarrhea on tucatinib/capecitabine, managed with dose reduction of capecitabine and loperamide.
At age 58, grade 2 hand-foot syndrome from capecitabine requiring topical emollients and brief treatment hold.
At age 58, restaging MRI brain after 3 months showed reduction in all 3 brain lesions. Partial intracranial response.
At age 58, CT showed stable pulmonary nodules and decreased liver lesion size. Partial systemic response.
At age 58, patient tolerating tucatinib combination with dose-modified capecitabine. ECOG 1. Headaches resolved after SRS. Continue current regimen.
At age 58, liquid biopsy (Guardant360) at recurrence detected ERBB2 amplification, PIK3CA N345S, ESR1 Y537S (likely acquired on aromatase inhibitor), and TP53 R248W.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 56, left breast stereotactic biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 2, 1.8 cm. ER positive 75%, PR weakly positive 15%, HER2 IHC 2+ with FISH amplified (HER2/CEP17 ratio 2.8, average HER2 copy number 8.2). Ki-67 35%.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 56, screening mammogram showed an irregular 1.7 cm mass in the left upper outer quadrant with associated architectural distortion, BIRADS 4C.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 56, staging CT showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease. MRI breast showed a single 1.9 cm enhancing mass with no additional suspicious lesions.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 57, routine follow-up. Patient on anastrozole, tolerating well with mild arthralgias. Physical exam unremarkable.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
At age 58, patient developed persistent headaches. MRI brain showed 3 enhancing lesions in the cerebellum and right frontal lobe (largest 1.8 cm), consistent with brain metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

FINDINGS:
At age 58, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed new bilateral pulmonary nodules and a single 2.2 cm liver lesion, confirming systemic recurrence.

Please correlate clinically.

FINDINGS:
At age 58, restaging MRI brain after 3 months showed reduction in all 3 brain lesions. Partial intracranial response.

Please correlate clinically.

FINDINGS:

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 58, patient tolerating tucatinib combination with dose-modified capecitabine. ECOG 1. Headaches resolved after SRS. Continue current regimen.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 58, liquid biopsy (Guardant360) at recurrence detected ERBB2 amplification, PIK3CA N345S, ESR1 Y537S (likely acquired on aromatase inhibitor), and TP53 R248W.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
